---
title: "flat_trend_correlation.Rmd"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r development, include=FALSE}
library(testthat)
```

```{r development-load}
# Load already included functions if relevant
pkgload::load_all(export_all = FALSE)
```

# cor_gam_trajectories

```{r function-cor_gam_trajectories}
#' Correlation between annual average values
#' 
#' Description
#' 
#' @param data 
#' @param group_vars 
#' @param parameter_col 
#' @param year_col 
#' @param value_col 
#' @param method 
#' @param min_n 
#'
#' @return
#' 
#' @noRd
cor_gam_trajectories <- function(gam_predictions,
                                 group_vars = c("estuary", "haline_zone", "season"),
                                 parameter_col = "PARAMETRE_LIBELLE",
                                 year_col = "year",
                                 fitted_col = "fitted_gam",
                                 method = "spearman",
                                 min_n = 6) {
  
  wide_data <- gam_predictions |>
    select(all_of(c(group_vars, year_col, parameter_col, fitted_col))) |>
    pivot_wider(
      names_from = all_of(parameter_col),
      values_from = all_of(fitted_col)
    )
  
  wide_data |>
    group_by(across(all_of(group_vars))) |>
    group_modify(~ {
      
      df <- .x |>
        select(-all_of(year_col))
      
      param_names <- names(df)
      
      if (length(param_names) < 2) {
        return(tibble())
      }
      
      pairs <- combn(param_names, 2, simplify = FALSE)
      
      map_dfr(pairs, function(pair) {
        
        x <- df[[pair[1]]]
        y <- df[[pair[2]]]
        
        ok <- complete.cases(x, y)
        
        if (sum(ok) < min_n) {
          return(tibble(
            parameter_1 = pair[1],
            parameter_2 = pair[2],
            n = sum(ok),
            correlation = NA_real_,
            p_value = NA_real_
          ))
        }
        
        test <- cor.test(
          x[ok],
          y[ok],
          method = method,
          exact = FALSE
        )
        
        tibble(
          parameter_1 = pair[1],
          parameter_2 = pair[2],
          n = sum(ok),
          correlation = unname(test$estimate),
          p_value = test$p.value
        )
      })
    }) |>
    ungroup() |>
    mutate(
      p_adjusted = p.adjust(p_value, method = "BH")
    )
}
```
  


```{r development-inflate, eval=FALSE}
# Run but keep eval=FALSE to avoid infinite loop
# Execute in the console directly
fusen::inflate(flat_file = "dev/flat_trend_correlation.Rmd", 
               vignette_name = NULL, overwrite = TRUE, check = FALSE)
```
